Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. MariaDB innovation: vector index performance 24 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Tony Hoare has died 972 upvotes, 142 comments
  3. Defeat as Method 15 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. The Enterprise Context Layer 24 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. PgAdmin 4 9.13 with AI Assistant Panel 76 upvotes, 21 comments
  6. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 178 upvotes, 61 comments
  7. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 179 upvotes, 62 comments
  8. I used pulsar detection techniques to turn a phone into a watch timegrapher 42 upvotes, 10 comments
  9. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 122 upvotes, 44 comments
  10. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" (2009) 248 upvotes, 104 comments
  11. I put my whole life into a single database 361 upvotes, 172 comments
  12. Open Weights Isn't Open Training 30 upvotes, 10 comments
  13. We are building data breach machines and nobody cares 35 upvotes, 13 comments
  14. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 385 upvotes, 237 comments
  15. FFmpeg-over-IP – Connect to remote FFmpeg servers 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  16. Google to Discontinue Widevine Cloud License Service in April 2027 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. Bypassing Apache Fop PostScript Escaping to Reach GhostScript 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  18. Billion-Parameter Theories 49 upvotes, 24 comments
  19. Meta acquires Moltbook 268 upvotes, 182 comments
  20. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 13 upvotes, 4 comments
  21. Rebasing in Magit 146 upvotes, 105 comments
  22. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 191 upvotes, 158 comments
  23. RFC 454545 – Human Em Dash Standard 94 upvotes, 78 comments
  24. You Hired the AI to Write the Tests. Of Course They Pass 7 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 312 upvotes, 324 comments
  26. Levels of Agentic Engineering 24 upvotes, 17 comments
  27. Throwing away 18 months of code and starting over 37 upvotes, 30 comments
  28. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 39 upvotes, 39 comments
  29. I built a programming language using Claude Code 64 upvotes, 83 comments
  30. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 103 upvotes, 249 comments

Last generated 3:30am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
